seoTitle: How to Track Your Mileage in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to set up and use mileage tracking in QuickBooks Online so every business trip is logged accurately for expense and tax purposes.
intro: Keeping an accurate mileage log matters for both expense tracking and tax deductions, and QuickBooks Online has a built-in way to handle it. This guide walks through turning on mileage tracking and logging trips correctly.

steps.heading: Turn on mileage tracking
steps.body: Go to the mileage section of QuickBooks Online and enable tracking for your account.

steps.heading: Add a vehicle
steps.body: Set up the vehicle you'll be using for business trips so mileage can be tied to it.

steps.heading: Log your trips
steps.body: Enter trips manually or use automatic tracking if you're on the mobile app, marking each trip as business or personal.

steps.heading: Review mileage reports
steps.body: Check your logged mileage periodically to make sure trips are categorized correctly for deductions.

faq.question: Can QuickBooks Online track mileage automatically?
faq.answer: The mobile app can track trips automatically using your phone's location, which you then classify as business or personal.

faq.question: How do I mark a trip as business versus personal?
faq.answer: Each logged trip has a toggle or setting to classify it, which determines whether it counts toward deductible mileage.

faq.question: Does mileage tracking work without the mobile app?
faq.answer: You can also manually enter trips through the web version if you'd rather not use the mobile app.

faq.question: Can I edit a mileage entry after saving it?
faq.answer: Yes, you can go back and edit trip details like distance or classification if something was entered incorrectly.
